content: About 40 to 50% of Americans are already deficient in vitamin D according to the National Library of Medicine.
Serotonin is a neurotransmitter responsible for feelings of well-being and calmness, and its production involves tryptophan, which requires protein consumption and carbohydrate intake.
Vitamin D plays a critical role in the conversion process of tryptophan to serotonin, assisting in the metabolism and creation of serotonin.

A study by Oregon State University found a direct relationship between low vitamin D levels and increased symptoms of depression and mood disorders in 185 female college students over the course of a year.
